geohashTools 0.3.3
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Helpers for interfacing geohashes with sf objects

Source: R/gis_tools.R
gis_tools.Rd

These functions smooth the gateway between working with geohashes and geospatial information built for the major geospatial package in R, sf::sf.

gh_to_sf(...)

# Default S3 method
gh_to_sf(geohashes, ...)

# S3 method for class 'data.frame'
gh_to_sf(gh_df, gh_col = "gh", ...)

gh_covering(x, precision = 6L, minimal = FALSE)

Arguments

...

Arguments for subsequent methods.

geohashes

character vector of geohashes to be converted to polygons.

gh_df

data.frame which 1) contains a column of geohashes to be converted to polygons and 2) will serve as the attribute table of the resultant sf::sf object.

gh_col

character column name saying where the geohashes are stored in gh_df.

x

An sf, sfc, or sfg object (from the sf package).

precision

integer specifying the precision of geohashes to use, same as gh_encode()

minimal

logical; if FALSE, the output will have all geohashes in the bounding box of x; if TRUE, any geohashes not intersecting x will be removed.

Value

For gh_to_sf, a sf::sf object.

Details

gh_to_sf relies on the gh_decode() function. Note in particular that this function accepts any length of geohash (geohash-6, geohash-4, etc.) and is agnostic to potential overlap, though duplicates will be caught and excluded.

Examples

# get the neighborhood of this geohash in downtown Apia as an sf object
downtown = '2jtc5x'
apia_nbhd = unlist(gh_neighbors(downtown))
apia_sf = gh_to_sf(apia_nbhd)

# all geohashes covering a random sampling within Apia:
apia_covering = gh_covering(sf::st_sample(apia_sf, 10L, type = 'random'))
#> Warning: coordinate ranges not computed along great circles; install package lwgeom to get rid of this warning

Contents
